message:
fcp-conformance: sign gossip summaries in the test harness; rustfmt mesh

TestMeshNode previously did not hold a signing identity; the TestHarness
delivered unsigned GossipSummary values directly to the target mesh's
gossip handle via gossip_mut().handle_summary(...), which bypassed the
peer-signature verification path that real MeshNode instances go through.
That skipped exactly the code under test: MeshNode::verify_summary_
signature, which requires a NodeSignature whose node_id matches the
summary source and whose bytes verify against a registered peer public
key.

Changes:

  - Each TestMeshNode now holds an Ed25519SigningKey generated at
    construction and exposes the matching verifying_key().
  - TestHarness::register_all_peer_signing_keys walks the node list and
    calls MeshNode::register_peer_signing_key for every (peer, key)
    pair on every node (except itself). It is invoked both after
    construction and after start_all, so restarted nodes are
    re-registered with the rest of the mesh.
  - The gossip delivery loop clones each pending summary, signs it with
    the source node's signing key, stamps a NodeSignature with the
    source node_id and timestamp, and delivers it via
    MeshNode::handle_gossip_message(GossipMessage::Summary, ...) rather
    than directly poking the gossip state. This exercises the full
    signature-verification → ingest path end-to-end.

crates/fcp-mesh/src/node.rs and crates/fcp-mesh/tests/
mesh_integration.rs are rustfmt-only reflows of gossip imports and
verify_summary_signature / verify_revocation_push_signature; no
semantic change.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
